message:
beads: close 8a2wv and cm3k1

Two beads transitioned from in_progress to closed — metadata catch-up
for beads whose fix work landed in earlier commits this session.

- flywheel_connectors-8a2wv: closed.
- flywheel_connectors-cm3k1: closed.

Close reasons are captured inline in each bead's JSONL entry. Not
replicated here because the tracker is the source of truth.

No source files changed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-84phy.2","title":"[FCP3/P7.5] Publish the final go-or-no-go review record for cutover","description":"Background: once the checklist is complete, the project still needs a durable go or no-go record that explains whether closure happened and why.

Scope: publish the final review record, including the go or no-go decision, remaining blockers if any, explicit references to the proof manifest sections, and the criteria that were used to allow or deny initiative closure.

Acceptance: future contributors can see exactly why the project was or was not declared finished, and what evidence supported that decision.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:59:24.288015Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:15:31.065474Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T16:15:31.064967Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["closure","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","phase-7","review"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-84phy.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-84phy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:59:24.288015Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-84phy.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-84phy.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:59:40.036901Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2443,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-84phy.2","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group closure note: this review behaves like a release gate. The unresolved-item matrix should enumerate blocker, evidence status, owner, rerun command, and decision needed; the go or no-go record should explicitly cite the proof manifest, placeholder audit, quarantine scoreboard, and benchmark comparison rather than summarizing them vaguely.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:24Z"},{"id":2774,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-84phy.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Published FCP3 go/no-go review record at docs/FCP3_Go_No_Go_Review_Record.md (commit e6474d92). Decision: GO (conditional). All 7 closure gates pass. Unresolved-item matrix has 4 non-blocking items with explicit owner beads. Future contributors can see exactly why the project was declared ready for closure and what evidence supports the decision.","created_at":"2026-04-19T16:11:51Z"},{"id":2778,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-84phy.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-19 re-verification: Go/No-Go record published at docs/FCP3_Go_No_Go_Review_Record.md. Decision GO (conditional). All 7 gates PASS. 4 non-blocking tracked items. Independent verification: 50 owner crate tests pass, 921 conformance lib tests pass, 78 conformance integration tests pass, workspace check clean. 2 pre-existing fwc test failures from other agents' in-flight changes (not cutover blockers). Re-closing.","created_at":"2026-04-19T16:15:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-86hnq","title":"[bug] fcp-raptorq: 6 golden erasure recovery tests fail with data mismatch","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CreamReef","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:33:51.946480Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-01T03:06:19.252590Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T21:43:26.308731Z","close_reason":"Stabilized decoder reconstruction order by storing received symbols in ascending ESI order; added reverse-arrival repair regression; light checks passed, rch test blocked by RCH-E326 and local fallback aborted immediately","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2067,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-86hnq","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak deep investigation on 2026-03-27: All 6 golden erasure recovery tests fail with garbled (not rotated) decoded data. The decoded payload has correct length but wrong content — intermediate symbols are solved incorrectly. Root cause is in the Gaussian elimination solver in ConstraintMatrix::solve() (systematic.rs:250-367). The minimum-degree pivot selection with row swapping has a suspected interaction between used_row tracking and physical row swapping, but a targeted fix to the swap sequence didn't resolve the issue. The constraint matrix setup (LDPC/HDPC/LT rows), GF(256) arithmetic, and RFC 6330 tuple expansion are all correct. The encoder works correctly. The InactivationDecoder's peeling + inactivation phases may have a subtle ordering bug when the system is underdetermined (missing source symbols). Next steps: (1) add diagnostic output to compare encoded intermediate symbols vs decoded ones, (2) test with trivial 2-symbol case to isolate minimum reproduction, (3) check if the constraint_symbols() properly extracts equations or if row ordering matters.","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:42:37Z"},{"id":2193,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-86hnq","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak fix on 2026-03-31: The 6 golden erasure recovery test failures are RESOLVED. Root cause was a use-after-move bug in asupersync's SystematicEncoder constructor (/dp/asupersync/src/raptorq/systematic.rs:888). The `params` struct was moved into `Self` at line 882 and then `params.k_prime` was read at line 888 to set `next_repair_esi`. This caused `next_repair_esi` to be garbage, which meant repair symbols were generated with wrong ESIs, making erasure recovery impossible. Fix: read `k_prime` before moving `params`. Full raptorq suite: 739/739 pass.","created_at":"2026-04-01T03:06:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-89yq1","title":"Implement perplexity-search connector: Perplexity search API","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:14:10.840541Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:50:03.240644Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:50:03.240489Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","hardening","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-89yq1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1i9ky","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:14:20.853817Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1964,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-89yq1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Perplexity Search API. Documented at https://docs.perplexity.ai.

Key operations: search query with citations. Simple REST API with API key auth. The scaffold declares planned operations. Implementation is straightforward: single search endpoint, structured response with citations and sources.","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:14:20Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-8a2wv","title":"fcp-host capability verification trusts rollbackable wall clock for expiry","description":"Capability verification in fcp-host derives temporal validity directly from `Utc::now()` and then treats that wall-clock result as authoritative for live auth.

Evidence:
- `crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs:3891-3912` computes `currently_valid` from `Utc::now()` with no monotonic floor or rollback guard.
- `crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs:4041-4050` re-checks not-before against another fresh `Utc::now()`.
- `crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:1509-1530` accepts `persisted_verify.valid` as a live execution gate.

Concrete failure:
1. Issue a capability token with a 60-second TTL.
2. Let it expire in real time.
3. Move the host clock backwards by 2 minutes.
4. `verify_capability_token` recomputes `currently_valid = true`, and `verify_live_request` admits an already-expired token.

This is a time-travel auth bypass. Expiry needs a monotonic last-seen floor, persisted max-observed time, or explicit rollback/skew rejection.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:00:00Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:17:14.845018Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auth","fcp-host","security","time"]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-8a2wv","title":"fcp-host capability verification trusts rollbackable wall clock for expiry","description":"Capability verification in fcp-host derives temporal validity directly from `Utc::now()` and then treats that wall-clock result as authoritative for live auth.

Evidence:
- `crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs:3891-3912` computes `currently_valid` from `Utc::now()` with no monotonic floor or rollback guard.
- `crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs:4041-4050` re-checks not-before against another fresh `Utc::now()`.
- `crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:1509-1530` accepts `persisted_verify.valid` as a live execution gate.

Concrete failure:
1. Issue a capability token with a 60-second TTL.
2. Let it expire in real time.
3. Move the host clock backwards by 2 minutes.
4. `verify_capability_token` recomputes `currently_valid = true`, and `verify_live_request` admits an already-expired token.

This is a time-travel auth bypass. Expiry needs a monotonic last-seen floor, persisted max-observed time, or explicit rollback/skew rejection.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:00:00Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:22:44.818033Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:22:44.817785Z","close_reason":"Fix landed in 1b63f2e2 (monotonic token-verification clock) + 983ec87e (apply_mutation comparison)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auth","fcp-host","security","time"]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8azf9","title":"[HIGH] Inconsistent CBOR tag rejection in fcp-crypto vs fcp-cbor","description":"crates/fcp-crypto/src/cose.rs:400 + fcp-crypto accepts CBOR tags in CwtClaims values, but fcp-cbor (the project standard) rejects them. This creates an interoperability failure where tokens verified by fcp-crypto cannot be decoded by fcp-cbor components, and allows non-canonical tokens into the system.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:39:18.448047Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:52:25.441086Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:52:25.440775Z","close_reason":"782f1341 fcp-crypto cose.rs:400 rejects CBOR tags in CwtClaims via recursive reject_cbor_tags walker; new CryptoError::InvalidCborTag variant mirrors fcp_cbor::SerializationError::UnsupportedTag","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8bfb","title":"TEST: Bootstrap/Genesis Unit Tests (First-Run, Owner Key Ceremony, Initial Enrollment)","description":"-","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberMeadow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:25:31.779434544Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:45:13.426966365Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:45:13.426877330Z","close_reason":"Completed bootstrap/ceremony/time validation tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap","fcp2","mvp","testing","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bfb","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bfb","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-atmv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","title":"[FCP3/P7.3] Produce the final conformance, performance, and operational proof bundle","description":"Background: the migration should end with a proof bundle, not a narrative claim. The project needs one place where conformance, performance, and operational evidence show that the new architecture really exists and remains within project goals.

Scope: assemble the final proof bundle covering semantic ownership, protocol and durable alignment, host and SDK convergence, mesh pilot behavior, CLI truthfulness, deletion backed final architecture shape, and relevant performance and operational characteristics. Explicitly gather the outputs of the preservation tests, cross crate conformance vectors, host backed integration harnesses, mesh failover coverage, CLI acceptance coverage, performance regression harnesses, structured logs, transcripts, and artifact bundles produced by earlier verification beads so the final review is grounded in reproducible evidence rather than summary prose.

Acceptance: cutover can be defended with concrete artifacts, detailed logs, runnable verification scripts, and explicit proof that the final post deletion architecture still satisfies user visible and operator visible requirements.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:09.335001Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:06:10.773904Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T16:06:10.773393Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","mesh-first","performance","phase-7","proof","tests","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0aczd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:30:07.870749Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-8jc8f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:56.898556Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-bz431","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:56.052930Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ciy20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:30:06.485857Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-qvsq8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:30:03.932820Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-si48n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:30:05.411778Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ukr33","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:30:08.755523Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-z1nkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:57.720816Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2391,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-8bqme","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Decomposed @@ -1152,7 +1152,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cdj","title":"fcp.1password: Credential Management Connector","description":"## Overview
Secure credential management connector for 1Password integration, enabling AI agents to safely access and manage secrets without exposing sensitive data.

## Core Capabilities

### Item Lookup (Read-Only by Default)
- Search vault items by name, tags, or categories
- Retrieve specific fields (username, URL, notes) without exposing passwords
- Read-only access as the default permission level
- Explicit elevation required for password retrieval

### Password Generation
- Generate strong passwords with configurable requirements
- Support for length, character sets, and complexity rules
- Memorable password generation (word-based)
- PIN generation for numeric-only contexts

### Entry Creation
- Create new login entries with generated passwords
- Support for secure notes, credit cards, identities
- Custom field support for application-specific data
- Automatic categorization and tagging

### Vault Organization
- List and navigate vault structure
- Move items between vaults
- Bulk tagging operations
- Archive and restore items

### Secure Note Access
- Read secure notes for configuration data
- Structured note parsing (YAML, JSON embedded)
- Document attachment retrieval
- Version history access

## Security Requirements

### Credential Protection
- **CRITICAL**: Never expose credentials in logs or output
- Mask all sensitive fields in debug output
- Use secure memory handling
- Clear credentials from memory after use

### Time-Limited Credential Leases
- Short-lived access tokens (5-minute default)
- Automatic credential rotation
- Lease renewal with re-authentication
- Audit trail for all credential access

## Implementation Notes
- Use 1Password CLI (op) or Connect API
- Support both personal and team accounts
- Service account integration for automation
- MFA handling for interactive sessions

## Testing Requirements
- Mock vault for unit tests
- Integration tests with test vault
- Security audit for credential handling
- Penetration testing for leak prevention","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:48.804778945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T07:34:56.145167808Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T07:34:56.145167808Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-8egw","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cga","title":"fcp.spotify: Podcast Episode Data Access","description":"# fcp.spotify: Podcast Episode Data Access (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide access to podcast metadata for discovery and summarization workflows.

## Scope
- Get show metadata.
- List show episodes (pagination).
- Get episode metadata.
- Optional: saved episodes list (ties to library mgmt).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.podcasts.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Treat titles/descriptions as tainted external content.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.show.get`
  - `spotify.show.episodes.list`
  - `spotify.episode.get`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - market handling

## Acceptance criteria
- Podcast metadata access is reliable and correctly typed.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:13.095708643Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T19:40:35.250132Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T19:40:35.250116Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cga","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cga","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-a4dh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1352,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cga","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented 3 podcast operations: show.get, show.episodes, episode.get. All with optional market parameter and pagination. Spotify at 42 ops total, 113 tests pass, golden vectors verified.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:40:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ciy20","title":"[FCP3/P4.5] Add host backed integration coverage across native and WASI execution forms","description":"Background: the platform must preserve one execution model across multiple execution forms. That claim needs host backed tests that exercise native and WASI sandboxed connector execution against the same semantics and produce enough detail to explain mismatches.

Scope: add integration coverage and scriptable end to end harnesses that prove the host, SDK, and kernel contract behaves consistently across local native and sandboxed WASI execution forms, including lifecycle, checkpoint, health, and evidence surfaces. Require structured lifecycle logs and artifact capture so a failure can be traced to a specific stage of the runtime model.

Acceptance: execution form neutrality is backed by comprehensive tests and detailed logs rather than by assumption.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:06.817941Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T19:02:30.456770Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T19:02:30.456461Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","integration","logging","mesh-first","phase-4","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ciy20","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:36.112373Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2243,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ciy20","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Added execution form neutrality integration tests in crates/fcp-host/tests/no_mock_integration.rs:

1. execution_form_health_vocabulary_is_unified: Verifies HealthState (Starting/Ready/Degraded/Error/Stopping) serializes deterministically across execution forms
2. execution_form_self_check_vocabulary_is_unified: Verifies SelfCheckReport (Ok/Degraded/Failed) serde roundtrip is form-neutral
3. execution_form_doctor_report_schema_stable: Verifies DoctorReport schema (status/version/zone_id/checkpoint/revocation/audit) is consistent
4. execution_form_connector_health_variants_cover_both_forms: Verifies ConnectorHealth (Healthy/Degraded/Unavailable) covers both native and WASI failure modes

Combined with existing coverage: 43+ no_mock_integration tests cover doctor, self_check, health, budget lifecycle, and discovery across the host. Telegram connector (306 tests) and Discord connector (524 tests) prove the E2E contract works for native execution. WASI sandbox has 543+ tests in fcp-sandbox. Together these prove execution form neutrality through the shared vocabulary.","created_at":"2026-04-01T19:02:25Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-cm3k1","title":"fcp-host not_before_delay overflow fails open to immediate token validity","description":"Capability issuance accepts `not_before_delay_secs: Option<u64>`, but the production path silently maps overflow to zero seconds instead of rejecting it.

Evidence:
- `crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs:650-665` exposes `not_before_delay_secs` as unbounded `u64`.
- `crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs:3719-3721` computes `not_before` with `i64::try_from(delay).unwrap_or(0)`.

Concrete failure:
1. Send an issuance request with `not_before_delay_secs = 18446744073709551615`.
2. The `u64 -> i64` conversion fails.
3. `unwrap_or(0)` collapses the delay to zero.
4. The minted token becomes valid immediately instead of being deferred or rejected.

This is a fail-open time gate in capability issuance; overflow should reject the request or clamp safely, not mint an immediately-usable token.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:00:00Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:17:13.237566Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auth","fcp-host","security","time"]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-cm3k1","title":"fcp-host not_before_delay overflow fails open to immediate token validity","description":"Capability issuance accepts `not_before_delay_secs: Option<u64>`, but the production path silently maps overflow to zero seconds instead of rejecting it.

Evidence:
- `crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs:650-665` exposes `not_before_delay_secs` as unbounded `u64`.
- `crates/fcp-host/src/admin_state.rs:3719-3721` computes `not_before` with `i64::try_from(delay).unwrap_or(0)`.

Concrete failure:
1. Send an issuance request with `not_before_delay_secs = 18446744073709551615`.
2. The `u64 -> i64` conversion fails.
3. `unwrap_or(0)` collapses the delay to zero.
4. The minted token becomes valid immediately instead of being deferred or rejected.

This is a fail-open time gate in capability issuance; overflow should reject the request or clamp safely, not mint an immediately-usable token.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:00:00Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:22:44.398611Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:22:44.398280Z","close_reason":"Fix landed in 1b63f2e2 (cap not_before_delay_secs) + 983ec87e (follow-up)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auth","fcp-host","security","time"]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cmkuk","title":"fcp-discord: invalid embeds input is silently discarded in send/edit paths","description":"## Finding
Both `invoke_send_message()` and `invoke_edit_message()` parse `input["embeds"]` with `serde_json::from_value(...).ok()` ([connectors/discord/src/connector.rs:1338-1346](connectors/discord/src/connector.rs:1338), [1448-1455](connectors/discord/src/connector.rs:1448)).

Because the deserialization error is discarded, malformed embed payloads are treated as `None` instead of producing a typed request error. In `send_message`, that falls through to the generic `Either `content` or `embeds` must be provided` branch; in `edit_message`, malformed embeds bypass embed validation entirely and the edit proceeds as though no embeds were supplied.

## Why this matters
Agents get misleading validation feedback and can accidentally send/edit messages after supplying malformed embeds. This is a silent input-shape downgrade, not strict request validation.

## Recommendation
Decode `embeds` with `map_err(...)` and return `FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1003/1004, message: ... }` that explicitly says the embeds payload is malformed.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:56:43.535144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:19:45.090339Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:19:45.089178Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2815,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cmkuk","author":"jemanuel","text":"Landed in d8572f12. Added parse_embeds() helper that maps serde_json errors to FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1003 } naming the embeds field; wired into both invoke_send_message and invoke_edit_message in place of the silent .ok() discard. Two regression tests (send_message_malformed_embeds_returns_typed_error, edit_message_malformed_embeds_returns_typed_error) cover wrong-shape string-vs-array and array-of-primitives-vs-array-of-objects on both paths. fcp-discord: 63/63 tests passing (61 prior + 2 new) via rch on /tmp/fcp-p7-t32.","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:19:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","title":"fcp.datadog: Datadog Observability Platform Connector","description":"# fcp.datadog - Datadog Observability Platform Connector (FCP2)

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 84/100** — Important Tier 2 connector (Observability family).

**Why Critical**: Observability is essential for autonomous operations. Agents that can understand system health, diagnose issues, and correlate incidents enable truly autonomous SRE workflows. Datadog is the **market leader in cloud observability**.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (Specialized + High Value)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 2 Productivity (weeks 5-8)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![
    ConnectorArchetype::Knowledge,    // Metrics, logs, traces queries
    ConnectorArchetype::Operational,  // Monitor management, incidents
    ConnectorArchetype::Streaming,    // Webhook events
]
```
- **Knowledge**: Query metrics, logs, traces, dashboards
- **Operational**: Monitor CRUD, incident management
- **Streaming**: Webhooks for alerts, incidents

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::Stateless
```
- **Stateless**: Query operations are independent
- Time-range based queries (no cursor)
- Monitor state managed by Datadog

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: Handles API credentials
- Memory isolation for key protection
- Capability-gated hostcalls

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    // Datadog API endpoints (region-specific)
    host_allow: vec\![
        "api.datadoghq.com".into(),       // US1
        "api.datadoghq.eu".into(),        // EU1
        "api.us3.datadoghq.com".into(),   // US3
        "api.us5.datadoghq.com".into(),   // US5
        "api.ap1.datadoghq.com".into(),   // AP1
    ],
    port_allow: vec\![443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
// Telemetry data is internal/trusted
metric_data.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::NONE;

// Log messages may contain user data
log_message.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;

// Webhook payloads are external
webhook_event.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `datadog.metrics.query` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.logs.search` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.traces.search` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.dashboards.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.monitors.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `datadog.monitors.write` | Dangerous | Alert configuration |
| `datadog.incidents.manage` | Dangerous | Incident lifecycle |
| `datadog.dashboards.write` | Risky | Dashboard changes |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Metrics & Monitoring
- Query metrics (Datadog query language)
- Access dashboards
- Alert management
- Anomaly detection

### Logging
- Log search and analysis
- Log pattern detection
- Error correlation

### APM & Tracing
- Distributed trace inspection
- Service dependency mapping
- Performance bottleneck identification

### Incident Management
- View and manage incidents
- Correlate with metrics/logs
- Postmortem data

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Show me CPU usage for the past hour"
"Find error logs from the checkout service"
"What traces show latency > 500ms?"
"List all triggered monitors"
"Create a monitor for high error rate"
"Get incident timeline"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Sentry Integration
**Unified error tracking:**
- Error correlation
- Performance impact analysis

### GitHub Integration
- Deploy events → metric correlation
- Release impact analysis

### Slack/Twilio Integration
- Alert escalation
- Incident notifications

### Linear/Jira Integration
- Incident → issue creation
- Performance issue tracking

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **REST API v1/v2**: Primary interface
- **Webhooks**: Alert and incident events

### Recommended Crates
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `serde_json` — JSON handling

### Authentication
```rust
// API Key + Application Key
DD-API-KEY: {api_key}
DD-APPLICATION-KEY: {app_key}
```

### Query Language
```
avg:system.cpu.user{env:production} by {host}
```

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:ops:datadog:full` | Full access | All operations |
| `z:ops:datadog:read` | Read-only | Queries only |
| `z:ops:datadog:monitors` | Monitors | Alert management |

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `knowledge` — Telemetry queries
- `operational` — Monitor management
- `streaming` — Webhook events

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `api.datadoghq.com:443` (region-specific)

### Credential Injection
- API key + App key via egress proxy
- Never persist credentials

---

## Capability Model

### Query Operations
- `datadog.metrics.query` — Metric queries
- `datadog.logs.search` — Log search
- `datadog.traces.search` — Trace search
- `datadog.dashboards.read` — Dashboard data

### Management Operations
- `datadog.monitors.read` — List monitors
- `datadog.monitors.write` — Create/update monitors (Dangerous)
- `datadog.incidents.manage` — Incident lifecycle (Dangerous)

### Admin Operations
- `datadog.dashboards.write` — Dashboard changes

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Query Metrics
```json
POST /api/v1/query
{
  "from": 1609459200,
  "to": 1609545600,
  "query": "avg:system.cpu.user{env:production}"
}
```

### Search Logs
```json
POST /api/v2/logs/events/search
{
  "filter": {
    "query": "service:checkout status:error",
    "from": "now-1h",
    "to": "now"
  }
}
```

### List Monitors
```
GET /api/v1/monitor
```

### Create Monitor
```json
POST /api/v1/monitor
{
  "name": "High Error Rate",
  "type": "metric alert",
  "query": "avg(last_5m):sum:errors{service:api} > 100",
  "message": "Error rate exceeded threshold"
}
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- Query construction
- Response parsing
- Time range handling
- Error handling

### Integration Tests
- Mock Datadog API responses
- Multi-query scenarios
- Webhook handling

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints
- Dangerous operation approval flow
- Audit logging

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real Datadog API calls in unit tests
- Metric queries work
- Log search works
- Trace search works
- Monitor CRUD works
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.1","title":"fcp.datadog: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"## Goal
Produce the full FCP2 manifest and capability map for this connector.

This bead is where we translate the connector’s functional surface (operations) into a **mechanically checkable** contract:
- operation schemas + schema IDs
- capability families per operation
- per-operation NetworkConstraints (default deny)
- sandbox profile requirements
- declared state model (stateless / singleton_writer / crdt)

## Required content (must be explicit)
- Operation list is exhaustive and stable for the current phase.
- Capability IDs DO NOT encode hostnames/ports.
- NetworkConstraints are minimal and per-operation.
- Risk tier for each operation (Safe / Dangerous).
- Explicit bounds: timeouts, max response sizes, pagination limits.

## Tests
- Add good/bad manifest examples for the compliance runner.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes static checks in `flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5`.
- Default-deny is enforced by manifest + capability requirements.
